description:
You are on‐call as an operations engineer who has been paged because the “payment_api”
    container must be taken out of rotation immediately.  
    The infrastructure team uses a very light-weight, file-based mock container
    runtime located at  

        /home/user/mock_docker/containers/

    Inside that directory every container has its own sub-directory.  
    Each sub-directory contains exactly one small text file called “status.txt”.
    The content of “status.txt” is always either the single word

        RUNNING

    or

        STOPPED

    … nothing else, not even trailing spaces.  
    The incident response policy for this environment requires two short steps:

    1. Mark the container as stopped  
       • Replace the entire content of
         /home/user/mock_docker/containers/payment_api/status.txt  
         with the single word

             STOPPED

       Do not create a new file; overwrite the existing one.

    2. Write a one-line triage log entry  
       • Make sure the directory

             /home/user/incident_logs/

         exists (create it if it does not).  
       • Append exactly one new line to the file

             /home/user/incident_logs/payment_api_incident.log

         The line must have *precisely* the following format (no extra spaces):

             CONTAINER:payment_api STATUS:STOPPED TIMESTAMP:2023-09-15T12:00:00Z

         Use the timestamp shown above verbatim so that automated tests can
         verify the result deterministically.

    Verification notes that the automated grader will check:

    • /home/user/mock_docker/containers/payment_api/status.txt  
      must now contain the single word “STOPPED” (in uppercase, with a single
      trailing newline permitted).

    • /home/user/incident_logs/payment_api_incident.log  
      must exist and its last non-empty line must be exactly:

         CONTAINER:payment_api STATUS:STOPPED TIMESTAMP:2023-09-15T12:00:00Z

    • No other files should be altered.

    You will need only 2–3 familiar shell commands
    (for example: cat/echo, mkdir, etc.) to finish the task.
truth:
<!--
        Initial filesystem layout provided to the agent
    -->
    /home/user/mock_docker/containers/ is pre-created and contains these two sub-directories:

        1. /home/user/mock_docker/containers/payment_api/
             └── status.txt     (with content: RUNNING\n)

        2. /home/user/mock_docker/containers/web_frontend/
             └── status.txt     (with content: RUNNING\n)

    /home/user/incident_logs/ does NOT exist at start-up.

    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected state **after** the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/mock_docker/containers/payment_api/status.txt  
       → file exists and contains exactly:  STOPPED\n

    2. /home/user/incident_logs/  
       → directory exists

    3. /home/user/incident_logs/payment_api_incident.log  
       → file exists and its last non-empty line is exactly:

          CONTAINER:payment_api STATUS:STOPPED TIMESTAMP:2023-09-15T12:00:00Z

       (If the agent writes earlier lines, those will be ignored by the grader;
        only the very last non-empty line must match.)

    No other files should be changed or created.
